name: PolecatReceivesHook
description: Application spec. Proves the substrate-layer Hook + work-coordination Polecat compose end-to-end after Polecat v0.2's port-surface expansion. The unified Polecat now requires SLING + MERGE_READY + ISSUE_OBSERVE bindings even when the application's interest is hook-receive (we kept HOOK_IN per the 'one polecat' rule). Project-level stub actors (Trigger fires Sling; Issue advances spontaneously from Hooked through Closed; Refinery acknowledges MergeReady) provide the minimum scaffolding so Polecat can reach the hook-receive state. The canaries demonstrate (a) the hook fires, (b) the polecat reaches HandlingHook (substrate boundary works), and (c) the polecat reaches Done (the full work-flow composition works).

actors.name: Trigger
actors.owner: Stub. Fires the Sling that hooks Pol1 onto the Issue.

actors.name: Issue
actors.owner: Stub Issue. Starts at Hooked so Polecat can advance to Working on Sling. Spontaneous transitions through InProgress to Closed let Polecat reach Done. Doesn't model the full Issue lifecycle; future Issue component will.

triples.event: polecat begins work: issue advances to InProgress

actors.name: Refinery
actors.owner: Stub. Receives MergeReady; doesn't process. Future Refinery component will.

triples.event: receives merge ready signal from polecat

invariants.description: Canary. SHOULD VIOLATE. PreCompactHook reaches Fired.
Canary. SHOULD VIOLATE. Pol1 reaches HandlingHook (the substrate-layer hook-receive boundary actually fires through composition).
Canary. SHOULD VIOLATE. Pol1 reaches Done via the full work-flow (Absent -> Working -> Done after Sling + Issue advances + MergeReady sent). Proves the full work-coordination composition works.
